items.title: Anthropic maps recursive self-improvement risks
items.oneliner: Anthropic says recursive self-improvement is no longer sci-fi; engineers should treat self-editing models as an imminent safety problem.

items.title: DeepSeek claims a precision lead
items.oneliner: DeepSeek V4 Pro beat GPT-5.5 Pro 38 to 33 on fresh precision tasks, raising the bar for schema-critical workflows.

items.title: OpenAI is merging ChatGPT and Codex
items.oneliner: OpenAI is turning ChatGPT into an agent-and-coding superapp, signaling that standalone AI tools are heading for bundling.

items.title: xAI reportedly trained on Claude outputs
items.oneliner: xAI allegedly used Claude outputs for coding training after access was cut, exposing how porous model-to-model data boundaries remain.

items.title: Config files are a supply chain blindspot
items.oneliner: Config files that execute code are still slipping past reviews, giving attackers a quiet path to AWS and GitHub credentials.

items.title: Breach disclosure delays are getting worse
items.oneliner: After 1,000 breaches, Troy Hunt says disclosure lag is worsening, which means users still learn about compromises far too late.

items.title: Hiring algorithms show hidden discrimination
items.oneliner: A study of 3.4 million applications found hiring systems can mask severe racial disparities that aggregate metrics completely miss.

items.title: Washington may buy into OpenAI
items.oneliner: The US government is reportedly discussing a 1 to 5 percent OpenAI stake, making AI policy look more industrial than regulatory.

items.title: Banks are cutting junior analyst classes
items.oneliner: Some banks are shrinking junior analyst hiring by two-thirds while adding AI talent, a labor shift product teams should not ignore.

items.title: Nvidia and LG are building AI factories
items.oneliner: Nvidia and LG are pairing humanoid robotics with AI factory infrastructure, showing where real-world deployment money is moving.
